name: Potentiometry (Ion-Selective Electrode)
description: Potentiometry measures an ion's activity at zero current: a selective membrane develops a potential against a reference electrode following the Nernst/Nikolsky equation, and after calibration against standards the measured EMF is converted to concentration.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Sample solution"]
    N2["Immerse ISE + reference"]
    N3["Selective membrane responds"]
    N4["Membrane potential develops"]
    N5["Measure cell EMF"]
    N6{"Nernstian response?"}
    N7["Correct for interferents"]
    N8["Calibrate vs standards"]
    N9["EMF -> activity"]
    N10["Ion concentration"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
